message:
chore: add Rust browser example to canonical WASM catalog, expand harness tests, beads sync

docs/wasm_canonical_examples.md: adds the maintained Rust-browser
consumer example as a new row in the canonical examples catalog table
(RUST-BROWSER-CONSUMER scenario) alongside vanilla, worker, TypeScript,
React, and Next.js examples. Documents the fixture location
(tests/fixtures/rust-browser-consumer), validation script, contract
markers (ready_phase, disposed_phase, completed_task_outcome,
cancel_event_count), and deterministic repro command. Adds the
Rust-browser fixture to the quick-reference command section.

tests/wasm_canonical_examples_harness.rs: expanded test coverage for
the canonical examples catalog.

.beads/issues.jsonl: synced issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"asupersync-1ky0b","title":"[AA][Formal] Cancellation protocol formalization","description":"## Goal
Formalize the cancellation protocol as a state machine with budgets and phase transitions.

## Why
Cancellation is a multi‑phase protocol (request→drain→finalize). We need proof‑level clarity.

## Deliverables
- Formal state machine spec with guards and budgets.
- Proof sketch of idempotence and bounded cleanup.
- Mapping from runtime transitions to spec.

## Acceptance
- Spec covers CancelRequested/Cancelling/Finalizing/Completed.
- Proves that all tasks eventually reach terminal outcomes under sufficient budgets.

## Dependencies
- Uses the core small‑step semantics.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:05:36.612171824Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T02:25:39.817956116Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T02:25:39.817887909Z","close_reason":"Formalized cancellation protocol with budgets, idempotence, bounded cleanup","external_ref":"bd-1b5t","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
